title: Static minimal version
paragraphs: This stage focuses on the core HTML/CSS structure and basic navigation. It is intentionally simple, so you can iterate on layout, spacing, and copy without build steps.
I kept this stage minimal on purpose, so we can validate the layout, typography, and content flow before introducing interactivity or any framework.

title: React: first steps
paragraphs: This is just to showcase how react can be added to any existing project in a simple way. In a nutshell: This is jsut an iteration from the minimal version (vanilla) to see how react works under the hood 🤓.
Caution! This is not the recommended way of working with react! In a real project it would be imperative to use bundlers like Vite.
listItems: It is much more performant (~ x100 times more)

title: React App (Vite)
meta: Foundation stage for learning React fundamentals.
paragraphs: This is the foundation of the project. It is meant to explore React core concepts and create our own routing in order to understand how fundamentals like routing and state management work without making use of any library yet (react-router / zustand).
listItems: Basic react hooks + custom hooks usage

title: React Router + Zustand
meta: Routing-first iteration with shared state patterns.
paragraphs: This stage expands the React app with client-side routing and a global store. It is focused on multi-view navigation, route-aware layouts, and shared UI state across pages.
listItems: React Router for navigation and nested routes
Zustand store for shared state (to be wired up)
Scaling the app shell beyond a single view

title: Node Intro
meta: Core Node.js exercises and native server basics.
paragraphs: This folder is a sandbox for understanding Node without any framework abstraction. It covers small scripts, file system operations, command-line tools, and a simple HTTP server built with native modules.
It works as the backend fundamentals layer before moving into Express and more structured API work.
listItems: Basic Node scripts and environment usage
A custom CLI to inspect files and directories
A lightweight users API built with node:http

title: Express API
meta: Backend layer with routes, validation, and tests.
paragraphs: This stage moves the backend from native Node experiments into an Express application with clearer project structure. It introduces middleware, route modules, controllers, schemas, and a jobs-focused API.
It is the server-side counterpart to the frontend app and helps test more realistic integration flows.
listItems: Integration tests with the Node test runner

title: Testing
paragraphs: This folder is used to validate user flows from the outside, interacting with the running app as a real browser would. It contains both classic Playwright tests and an AI-assisted Stagehand experiment.
The goal is to compare different testing approaches while the app continues to evolve.
listItems: A separate workspace focused only on testing workflows
